title: Optimization of Production Processes using BPMN and ArchiMate
journal_or_publisher: International Journal of Advanced Computer Science and Applications
abstract: This article maps and optimizes production processes by combining BPMN and ArchiMate notation. It analyzes existing production processes in a Czech manufacturing company, proposes optimized processes with ERP implementation, documents the As-Is and To-Be states, and uses process-based simulation to quantify improvement effects.
keywords: Production processes; graphic modelling; BPMN; ArchiMate
methodology: Business process analysis; BPMN and ArchiMate modelling; As-Is/To-Be process redesign; ERP-oriented process optimization; process-based simulation.
main_findings: The paper proposes a combined BPMN-ArchiMate approach for production-process optimization and shows that process redesign supported by ERP and simulation can improve production-process efficiency.
notes: STYLE SAMPLE: This paper deals with the use of ArchiMate and BPMN languages for business architecture modelling.

title: Computer aided detection of breathing disorder from ballistocardiography signal using convolutional neural network
authors: Dalibor Cimr; Filip Studnicka; Hamido Fujita; Hana Tomaskova; Richard Cimler; Jitka Kuhnova; Jan Slegr
abstract: The paper proposes an unobtrusive computer-aided diagnostic system for detecting sleep-related breathing disorders from ballistocardiography signals. BCG sensors located on a bed capture breathing activity; Cartan curvatures extract significant signal information; and a 9-layer convolutional neural network classifies regular and disordered breathing samples.
keywords: Disordered breathing; Ballistocardiography; Cartan curvature; Convolutional neural networks; Computer-aided diagnostic; Tensometers
methodology: Unobtrusive BCG sensing; Cartan curvature feature extraction; 9-layer deep convolutional neural network; classification on regular and disordered breathing samples.
main_findings: The proposed CAD system achieved 98.00% average accuracy, 94.26% sensitivity, and 99.22% specificity on the evaluated dataset.
notes: STYLE SAMPLE: We achieved an average accuracy of 98.00%, sensitivity of 94.26%, and specificity of 99.22% on 4009 regular and 1307 disordered breathing samples.

title: An Agent-Based Simulation to Minimize Losses during a Terrorist Attack
abstract: The paper creates an AnyLogic agent-based model simulating part of the 1995 Tokyo subway sarin attack. The model is intended to support further investigation of the attack and to test what-if scenarios for minimizing losses and proposing future security options.
keywords: agent-based model; AnyLogic; terrorist attack; simulation
methodology: Agent-based simulation in AnyLogic; scenario modelling; what-if experiments; model validation against available information; statistical comparison of scenarios.
main_findings: The study demonstrates a simulation model of the Kodemmacho subway-station attack context and uses what-if scenarios to explore measures that could reduce losses in similar incidents.
notes: STYLE SAMPLE: The model should serve as a tool for further investigation of the attack and proposes the appropriate security options in the future.

title: Approaches combining methods of Operational Research with Business Process Model and Notation: A systematic review
abstract: This systematic literature review analyzes the use of operational research methods in conjunction with BPMN process models. It searches Web of Science for English full-text studies from 2004 to 2020, applies inclusion criteria focused on OR methods and BPMN use, and extracts methodological and bibliometric information.
keywords: BPMN; Business process model and notation; Operation Research; OR; Decision making; Techniques; Review
methodology: Systematic literature review; Web of Science search; inclusion/exclusion screening; bibliometric analysis; coding of OR techniques used with BPMN.
main_findings: The review included 36 studies and identified 11 operational-research method groups used with BPMN, concluding that OR methods can complement BPMN-based process analysis and reengineering.
notes: STYLE SAMPLE: Operational research methods are a useful complement to BPMN process analysis.

title: An integrated multi-criteria decision-making approach to optimize the number of leagile-sustainable suppliers in supply chains
authors: Saeid Sadeghi Darvazeh; Farzaneh Mansoori Mooseloo; Hadi Rezaei Vandchali; Hana Tomaskova; Erfan Babaee Tirkolaee
abstract: The study develops an integrated approach for determining the optimal number of suppliers in complex global supply chains. It combines leagile and environmental sustainability criteria with MCDM and simulation and applies the method in an empirical case study in an Iranian oil company.
keywords: Supply chain management; Optimal number of suppliers; Leagile-sustainable suppliers; BWM; SAW; TOPSIS
methodology: Integrated MCDM and simulation; BWM for weighting; SAW and TOPSIS for evaluation; scenario analysis; empirical case study in an Iranian oil company; sensitivity analysis.
main_findings: The study identifies the 1-1-1 supplier arrangement as the best scenario in the case study and demonstrates how leagile and sustainability criteria can support supplier-number optimization.
notes: STYLE SAMPLE: The final results represent that the scenario with a 1-1-1 arrangement is the best possible scenario.

title: Evaluation of Wind Turbine Failure Modes Using the Developed SWARA-CoCoSo Methods Based on the Spherical Fuzzy Environment
authors: Saeid Jafarzadeh Ghoushchi; Sepideh Miralizadeh Jalalat; Shabnam Rahnamay Bonab; Ali Memarpour Ghiaci; Gholamreza Haseli; Hana Tomaskova
abstract: The paper proposes a modified FMEA method for identifying and prioritizing potential wind-turbine failures. It uses spherical fuzzy sets to capture vagueness in expert information
keywords: applies SFS-SWARA to weight RPN criteria
methodology: and ranks failure modes with SFS-CoCoSo in a Manjil wind farm case study.
main_findings: CoCoSo SWARA; failure mode; multi criteria decision making; spherical fuzzy sets; wind turbines
notes: Modified FMEA; spherical fuzzy sets; SFS-SWARA criterion weighting; SFS-CoCoSo ranking; wind-farm case study; comparison with common FMEA and MCDM approaches.
column13: The proposed spherical fuzzy SWARA-CoCoSo FMEA model is presented as more realistic and reliable for prioritizing wind-turbine failures than common FMEA or other integrated MCDM approaches.
column14: STYLE SAMPLE: The concept of spherical fuzzy sets is utilized to address the vagueness and impreciseness of the information.

title: Business process optimization for trauma planning
authors: Hana Tomaskova; Erfan Babaee Tirkolaee; Rakesh Dulichand Raut
abstract: The study applies business process analysis and reengineering to trauma-planning documentation. It converts procedural documents into process models
methodology: and redesigns procedures to support better managerial decision-making and process efficiency.
main_findings: Process optimization; Business Process Model and Notation (BPMN); Trauma planning; Business process re-engineering; To-Be model; As-Is model
notes: Systematic literature review; documentary analysis; BPMN As-Is modelling; business process re-engineering; To-Be modelling; time-efficiency comparison.
column13: The study indicates that procedural process modelling and redesign can improve trauma-planning workflow efficiency while preserving the time required for partial activities.
column14: STYLE SAMPLE: Our study emphasizes the importance of considering the effectiveness of existing procedures and reengineering them where necessary.

title: A dynamic expert system to increase patient satisfaction with an integrated approach of system dynamics, ISM, and ANP methods
authors: Javad Nazarian-Jashnabadi; Shabnam Rahnamay Bonab; Gholamreza Haseli; Hana Tomaskova; Mostafa Hajiaghaei-Keshteli
abstract: The paper develops a dynamic expert system for patient satisfaction by integrating system dynamics, ISM, and ANP. It structures the problem with ISM, formulates and simulates the system in Vensim, validates behavior and sensitivity, and uses ANP to identify important criteria and support policy analysis.
keywords: Patient satisfaction; Health system; Dynamic expert system; ISM; ANP
methodology: System dynamics modelling; Interpretive Structural Modeling; Analytic Network Process; Vensim simulation; behavior reproduction and sensitivity validation; policy simulation.
main_findings: The results place criteria on three levels, identify causal relationships and leverage points, and show how ISM and ANP support system-dynamics-based patient-satisfaction policy design.
notes: STYLE SAMPLE: The ISM and ANP methods have an interesting synergy with the system dynamics approach and help identify leverage points.

title: Sustainable resilient supplier selection for IoT implementation based on the integrated BWM and TRUST under spherical fuzzy sets
authors: Shabnam Rahnamay Bonab; Gholamreza Haseli; Hamed Rajabzadeh; Saeid Jafarzadeh Ghoushchi; Mostafa Hajiaghaei-Keshteli; Hana Tomaskova
journal_or_publisher: Decision Making: Applications in Management and Engineering
abstract: The study develops an integrated spherical fuzzy MCDM approach for sustainable and resilient IoT supplier selection. It weights resilience and sustainability criteria with SFS-BWM and evaluates 14 IoT suppliers with SFS-TRUST, using comparison and sensitivity analysis to assess performance and ranking stability.
keywords: Supplier Selection; Sustainability; IoT; Spherical fuzzy sets; Best-Worst Method; TRUST
methodology: Spherical fuzzy BWM; spherical fuzzy TRUST; sustainable and resilient supplier-selection criteria; evaluation of 14 IoT suppliers; comparative analysis; sensitivity analysis.
main_findings: Pollution control and risk-taking are identified as the first and second priority sub-criteria, and the proposed SFS-TRUST ranking is reported as stable across scenarios.
notes: STYLE SAMPLE: The results of this study shows that the pollution control and risk-taking sub-criteria are placed in the first and second priorities, respectively.

title: Exploring decision-making techniques for evaluation and benchmarking of energy system integration frameworks for achieving a sustainable energy future
authors: Mohammed Taha Aljburi; A.S. Albahri; O.S. Albahri; A.H. Alamoodi; Saleh Mahdi Mohammed; Muhammet Deveci; Hana Tomaskova
abstract: The study addresses the multi-criteria complexity of evaluating and benchmarking Energy Systems Integration frameworks. It builds a dynamic decision matrix of 26 ESI frameworks and integrates FWZIC and MABAC to weight six ESI characteristics and rank the alternatives based on expert preferences.
keywords: Energy system integration; Sustainable energy; Fuzzy sets; MABAC; Dynamic selection; FWZIC
methodology: Dynamic Decision Matrix; FWZIC criterion weighting; MABAC benchmarking; 12-expert preference analysis; systematic rank and sensitivity assessments; benchmark comparison.
main_findings: Multivectoral and multidimensional criteria receive the highest weights, and the Energy Security and Energy Security under decarbonization frameworks rank first in the MABAC benchmarking.
notes: STYLE SAMPLE: This methodology offers valuable support in making informed and sustainable decisions in the energy sector.

title: Ranking Factors Affecting Sustainable Competitive Advantage From the Business Intelligence Perspective: Using Content Analysis and F-TOPSIS
authors: Arman Kazemi; Zohre Kazemi; Hamed Heshmati; Javad Nazarian-Jashnabadi; Hana Tomaskova
journal_or_publisher: Journal of Soft Computing and Decision Analytics
abstract: The study ranks factors affecting sustainable competitive advantage from a business-intelligence perspective. It first extracts indicators from previous studies by content analysis and then ranks the indicators using F-TOPSIS, grouping factors into five main criteria and 27 sub-criteria.
keywords: Sustainable Competitive Advantage; Business Intelligence; Brand trust; Social responsibility; Content Analysis; F-TOPSIS
methodology: Two-stage qualitative and quantitative design; content analysis of previous studies; F-TOPSIS ranking; criteria and sub-criteria classification.
main_findings: Customer relationship management is ranked as the most important main criterion, and effective interaction with customers is the most important sub-criterion within that category.
notes: STYLE SAMPLE: The aim of this study is to rank the factors influencing sustainable competitive advantage from a business intelligence standpoint.

title: Risk Management and Process Optimization in Industry 4.0: Integrating Sensors with Critical Path and FMEA
authors: Hana Tomaskova; Andrea Zvackova; Patrik Urbanik; Dominik Palla
journal_or_publisher: From Data to Models and Back: 12th International Symposium, DataMod 2024, Revised Selected Papers; Lecture Notes in Computer Science
abstract: The chapter presents an Industry 4.0 process-risk approach that integrates FMEA, CPM, BPMN, and sensor data. It applies the approach to a pallet manufacturing process and compares outcomes with and without sensor integration to assess risk and efficiency improvements.
keywords: BPMN; Industry 4.0; Process risk analysis; CPM
methodology: Hybrid FMEA-CPM approach; BPMN production-line modelling; sensor-based monitoring; comparison of scenarios with and without sensor integration; RPN and process-efficiency evaluation.
main_findings: Sensor integration in the hybrid process model reduces product-error risk at critical stages and reports more than 80% RPN reduction and more than 80% process-efficiency improvement.
notes: STYLE SAMPLE: Sensor data were used for quality control at critical stages of the manufacturing process.

title: Security modules of delegation methods in mobile cloud computing using probabilistic interval neutrosophic hesitant fuzzy set based decision-making model
authors: Sendeyah Al Hantoobi; A.A. Zaidan; Hassan Abdulsattar Ibrahim; Sarah Qahtan; Muhammet Deveci; Sinan Isik; Hana Tomášková
abstract: The paper proposes a decision-modeling approach to select effective security modules for mobile cloud computing delegation methods. It develops PINHFS-FWZIC for weighting security criteria and integrates it with EDAS to evaluate 50 security modules across four delegation-method categories and 13 development security criteria.
keywords: EDAS; FWZIC; mobile cloud computing; multi-criteria decision-making; probabilistic interval neutrosophic hesitant fuzzy set
methodology: Probabilistic interval neutrosophic hesitant fuzzy sets; FWZIC criterion weighting; EDAS ranking; decision-matrix construction; sensitivity analysis; Spearman correlation; comparative analysis.
main_findings: Data Security is identified as the most critical criterion, and the approach ranks optimal security modules for layering, encryption, dynamic offloading, and authentication delegation methods.
notes: STYLE SAMPLE: The results confirm that the proposed approach provides a comprehensive and reliable method for selecting optimal security modules in MCC delegation methods.

title: Economic production quantity model with shortages under price- and green-sensitive demand in uncertain environment
authors: Alay Kumar Mukherjee; Sankar Kumar Roy; Rajeswari Seshadri; Tomaskova Hana; Shawei He
abstract: The study extends the classical EPQ model for deteriorating items under uncertainty. It includes preservation technology, price- and green-sensitive demand, shortages with partial backordering, carbon emission taxation under four policies, development cost, inflation, and fuzzy-random cost parameters.
keywords: Economic production quantity; Price- and green-sensitive demand; Carbon emission; Development cost; Fuzzy-random number
methodology: Mathematical EPQ modelling; preservation-technology investment; price- and green-sensitive demand; shortage and partial-backordering modelling; carbon tax policy modelling; fuzzy-random uncertainty; hypothetical case studies; sensitivity analysis.
main_findings: The results suggest that a sustainable model incorporating controllable emissions, preservation technology, and green investment is more realistic and profitable than selected existing models.
notes: STYLE SAMPLE: This study advances the classical economic production quantity model by incorporating multiple real-world complexities within the context of deteriorating items under uncertain environment.

title: A hybrid decision support system for transport policy selection: A case study on Russia’s Northern Sea route in Artic region
authors: Galip Cihan Yalçın; Elif Gürdal Limon; Karahan Kara; Onur Limon; Pınar Gürol; Muhammet Deveci; Özgür Demirayak; Hana Tomášková
abstract: The study develops a hybrid decision support system for selecting transport policies and applies it to Russia’s Northern Sea Route in the Arctic region. The IF-SIWEC-ARLON method incorporates expert contributions, criteria weights, and transport-policy ranking under intuitionistic fuzzy sets.
keywords: Transport policy; Arctic region; Intuitionistic Fuzzy sets; Simple weight calculation; Alternative ranking using two-step logarithmic normalization
methodology: IF-SIWEC-ARLON hybrid DSS; intuitionistic fuzzy sets; expert contribution calculation; criteria weighting; policy alternative ranking; case study; sensitivity and comparative analysis.
main_findings: The best policy alternative is improved regulatory control with strategic international partnerships, and the most influential criterion is state interests, security, and sovereignty.
notes: STYLE SAMPLE: The selection of transport policies by countries is critical both for international relations and for the implementation of effective and sustainable transport practices.

title: Cloud supply chain as a service: overcoming barriers in the fashion retail industry by developing a new cognitive map model
authors: Gholamreza Haseli; Mostafa Hajiaghaei-Keshteli; Muhammet Deveci; Hana Tomaskova
abstract: The paper proposes a cloud supply chain as a service model for fashion retail and analyzes implementation barriers. It develops a cognitive map model based on system-of-systems theory and two-step group decision analytics and applies it to a sports fashion retailer in Dublin.
keywords: Cloud supply chain; Fashion industry; System of systems; Fuzzy cognitive maps; Group decision analytics
methodology: System-of-systems-based cognitive map model; two-step group decision analytics; expert and decision-maker judgments; barrier identification and positioning; reliability assessment; predictive scenarios; retail case study.
main_findings: The study identifies six main and 22 sub-barriers and provides a roadmap and predictive scenarios for overcoming barriers to cloud supply chain as a service in fashion retail.
notes: STYLE SAMPLE: Overall, the proposed cognitive map model provides actionable insights for implementing cloud supply chain as a service in fashion retail.

title: Comparative Analysis of AI-Integrated and Traditional Inventory Models under Uncertainty
authors: Alay Kumar Mukherjee; Tomaskova Hana; Sankar Kumar Roy
abstract: The chapter develops and compares two inventory models under uncertainty: one integrating AI and one without AI. Demand depends on customer experience and promotion level, both improved through AI; the model also includes item deterioration, preservation technology, carbon emissions, green technology, inflation, and fuzzy-random uncertainty.
keywords: Fuzzy-random number; Industry 4.0; AI; Carbon emission; Promotion; Green technology
methodology: Comparative inventory modelling; AI-enabled and traditional model formulation; fuzzy-random uncertainty; deterioration and preservation-technology modelling; emission and green-technology modelling; inflation; numerical example.
main_findings: The numerical example indicates that the AI-enabled model outperforms the traditional model and helps identify an optimal AI investment level balancing operational and sustainability goals.
notes: STYLE SAMPLE: Numerical example indicates that AI-enabled model is better than traditional model. Book chapter — DOI not assigned yet; verify bibliographic details before submission.
